channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: The Psychometric Properties of the Trunk Impairment Scale Used After Stroke: A Systematic Review Using COSMIN Methodology.
item.description: This systematic review, utilizing COSMIN methodology, evaluated the psychometric properties of the Trunk Impairment Scale (TIS) in individuals with stroke. Searching five electronic databases, it aimed to clarify the quality and certainty of evidence regarding TIS's measurement properties, which were previously unclear despite its wide use.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Critically appraised paper: Mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as and health status more than centre-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease [synopsis].
item.description: This critically appraised paper (CAP) highlights that mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as, and health status more than, center-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Cross-Education Effect on Force Production Capacity After Unilateral Resistance Training in Clinical Populations: A Systematic Review with Meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review with meta-analysis found a significant cross-education effect (force production gains in the contralateral non-trained limb) after unilateral resistance training in clinical populations, including those with musculoskeletal conditions and hemiparesis after stroke.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of RCTs found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction (LLE-BFR) provides additional benefits for muscle strength compared to conventional low-load (LLE) or high-load exercise (HLE) in individuals with musculoskeletal conditions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: A prospective RCT in Switzerland evaluated diagnostic and treatment agreement between physiotherapists and resident physicians compared to senior physicians for musculoskeletal conditions in the emergency department, assessing the feasibility of advanced physiotherapy practice roles.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: A study developed a core set of nine patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and corresponding measures (PROMs) for pediatric physical therapy practice, research, and quality assessment, covering functioning and other domains.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Physiotherapist-led treatment for femoroacetabular impingement syndrome (the PhysioFIRST study): an assessor-blinded, limited disclosure randomised controlled trial
item.description: An assessor-blinded RCT found that a targeted strengthening program (STRENGTH) led by physiotherapists was superior to a standardized stretching program (STRETCH) for femoroacetabular impingement syndrome (FAI) regarding hip-related quality of life and patient-perceived global change at 6 months.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Comparative effectiveness of physical therapy interventions in adults with rotator cuff tendinopathy: a systematic review and network meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and network meta-analysis identified the most effective physical therapy interventions for rotator cuff tendinopathy, suggesting a combination of manual therapy, exercise, and education as optimal.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Patterns of Outcome Measure Utilization in Stroke Rehabilitation Lower Extremity Trials Conducted in Low-to-Middle versus High-Income Countries.
item.description: This review examined outcome measure (OM) utilization patterns in 1,548 randomized controlled trials (RCTs) of post-stroke lower extremity motor rehabilitation across low-to-middle income (LMICs) and high-income countries (HICs). It found that LMICs tended to use more generalized impairment and activities of daily living OMs that rely on patient observation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Anti-Compensatory Saccades Changes After Visuo-Vestibular Physical Therapy in People With Acute Unilateral Vestibulopathy: A Prospective Observational Study.
item.description: This prospective observational study analyzed changes in anti-compensatory saccades (AcS) following an 8-week structured visuo-vestibular physical therapy (VVPT) program in 92 patients with acute unilateral vestibulopathy (AUVP). Assessments included the video Head Impulse test (vHIT- HIMP and SHIMP) at baseline and post-intervention.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Prioritising fall prevention strategies in Parkinson's disease: A multi-method evaluation.
item.description: This multi-method study integrated a structured narrative review, expert consensus via a two-round Delphi process with 15 multidisciplinary Parkinson's disease experts, and multi-criteria decision-making using the Best-Worst Method. It aimed to identify and quantitatively prioritize fall prevention strategies for people with Parkinson's disease.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Associations of neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing with rehabilitation outcomes in patients with stroke.
item.description: This prospective observational study examined the frequency of neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing in patients with stroke and their associations with post-treatment rehabilitation outcomes. Patients with post-stroke hemiplegia undergoing inpatient rehabilitation were evaluated at baseline and after a 4-week rehabilitation program.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: This exploratory retrospective cohort study of 333 adult patients undergoing elective ACDF from 2012-2022 determined that sarcopenia increased the risk of adjacent segment degeneration (ASD) within 3 years post-surgery. Sarcopenia was assessed using cross-sectional muscle and vertebral body measurements at C4 mid-vertebral level.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Shortwave diathermy for managing knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
item.description: This systematic review with meta-analysis, encompassing 17 trials and 1,372 participants, evaluated shortwave diathermy (SWD) for knee osteoarthritis (KOA). It found SWD significantly improved pain (mean -2.13; 95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]; p < .01) and walking ability (mean -0.72; 95% CI [-1.02, -0.42]; p < .01).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Management and implementation of intermittent catheterisation in neurogenic lower urinary tract dysfunction: a clinical practice guideline in Germany – update 3.0 | International Urology and Nephrology | Springer Nature Link
item.description: An update (3.0) to the clinical practice guideline in Germany on the management and implementation of intermittent catheterization in neurogenic lower urinary tract dysfunction has been published.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Fusogenic therapy restored spinal cord function after complete transection - Spinal Surgery News
item.description: An international team of researchers has restored motor, sensory, and pelvic functions after complete spinal cord transection in animal models using a combined fusogenic therapy and comprehensive rehabilitation protocol.
item.author: Spinal Surgery News

item.title: Prevention strategies and modifiable risk factors for upper extremity injury: a systematic review and meta-analysis for the female, woman and girl Athlete Injury pRevention (FAIR) consensus | British Journal of Sports Medicine
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis on prevention strategies and modifiable risk factors for upper extremity injury in female athletes contributes to the Female, Woman and Girl Athlete Injury Prevention (FAIR) consensus.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Improved Flexor Spasticity in Patients with Chronic Stroke Using a Dynamic Wrist-Hand Stretching Device: A Pilot Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A pilot RCT found that a dynamic wrist-hand stretching device improved flexor spasticity and hand function in chronic stroke patients, suggesting it as a potential adjunct therapy.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Efficacy and Modulating Factors of Repetitive Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation on Motor Function in Parkinson's Disease: A Meta-Analysis.
item.description: A meta-analysis found repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) to be effective for improving motor function in Parkinson's disease patients, short-term and long-term, with factors like stimulation site and frequency influencing outcomes.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Establishing weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury: a systematic review and methodological quality analysis.
item.description: A systematic review identified the need for weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injuries due to high recurrence rates and analyzed the methodological quality of existing literature, highlighting a need for higher-quality research.
item.author: PubMed
